crossRefs.label: Remove air cleaner (for vehicles without turbo charger)

blocks.text: Warm up the radiator fan until it operates twice. Place the vehicle on a level surface and stop the engine.
With the vehicle in a horizontal position, remove the drain bolt (A) and drain the transmission fluid.
4. Remove air cleaner (for vehicles without turbo charger)

refs.label: Remove air cleaner (for vehicles without turbo charger)

blocks.text: Remove the filler cap (A) and inject the specified Honda Genuine Ultra HMMF through the opening (B). Designation Transmission Fluid Honda Genuine Ultra HMMF Transmission Fluid quantity 2WD vehicle At exchange: 1 2.0L During overhaul 5.1L 4WD vehicle At exchange: 1 2.0L During overhaul 5.25L

items: Be careful to prevent dust and other debris from entering the transmission.
The filler cap must be installed with the "FRONT" mark (arrow) (C) facing toward the front of the vehicle.

blocks.text: 7. Air cleaner installed (for vehicles not equipped with turbo charger)

refs.label: Air cleaner installed (for vehicles not equipped with turbo charger)

items: The air bleeding of transmission fluid (HMMF) must be performed whenever there is a risk of air entering the control system oil circuit, such as when replacing parts that involve the removal and installation of transmission or control system parts.

blocks.text: Set the body on the lift so that all wheels can rotate freely. Activate VSA maintenance mode. In the D position, slowly depress the accelerator pedal and accelerate to a speed of approximately 60 km/h or more over a period of at least 10 seconds. Return the accelerator pedal and wait until the speed reaches the creep speed. Repeat steps 3-4 five times.

items: Do not step on the brake pedal.
